description: Wave 10 - Role Expansion & Multi-Context Scoring (DRAFT). Extends the scientific Mafia benchmark with deterministic scum-skill roles (deception/info-denial), a formal Night Action Resolution (NAR) tier system, and a multi-context rating architecture in which EVERY mode is SCORED but canonical 2-faction ELO ranking PURITY is firewalled and CI-enforced. Sequenced AFTER the still-unexecuted Wave 9 + v9-fixes (US-121..170) land; this wave is about the core benchmark engine, largely orthogonal to the human-multiplayer layer. Incorporates a 4-model review (Claude synthesis + mafiascum research agents + Codex gpt-5.5 + Antigravity Gemini-3.1-Pro). Guiding refinement from review: 'scored' must NOT mean 'same leaderboard' - each mode rates into its own context; canonical ladders stay narrow, boring, heavily tested. DRAFT: not activated, not run.

userStories.title: RatingContext abstraction + per-context rating tables (fail-closed)
userStories.description: Introduce a first-class RatingContext so every ruleset/mode maps to exactly one rating context, and ratings are written per-context. This is the substrate for 'every mode scored, canonical pure'. Context kinds: CANONICAL_TEAM (2-faction OpenSkill team rating), PLACEMENT (OpenSkill multi-team placement), SOLO_RATE (per-role success-rate metric). Canonical purity is preserved by strict separation of write paths, mirroring the existing scientific-vs-humans-included segregation.
acceptanceCriteria: New RatingContext concept: a rating_contexts table (id, kind in {CANONICAL_TEAM, PLACEMENT, SOLO_RATE}, ruleset_id, setup_id, is_canonical bool, display_label) and a rating_context_id FK on every rating write path (Rating/RatingEvent and the new sibling tables). One migration after the current head, linear.
Rulesets declare a canonical flag and a rating-context kind; get_ruleset exposes them. A ruleset with kind != CANONICAL_TEAM (or is_canonical=false) can NEVER resolve to a canonical context.
FAIL CLOSED: a game with a missing/unknown/None rating context is treated as UNRATED (written nowhere) - it NEVER defaults into a canonical ladder.
Canonical team rating writes carry: ruleset_id, setup_id, rating_context_id, every participant's model/build id, the game seed, and the final 2-team outcome.
Backfill existing mini7_v1/bench10_v1 ratings into their canonical contexts without changing any existing scientific ELO value (a test asserts byte-stable canonical ratings pre/post migration).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Generalizes the planned three rating contexts (scientific / humans-included / human-personal) into N contexts keyed by mode. Reuse the segregation pattern from US-125/US-146.

userStories.title: Canonical-purity invariant + CI gate (introspect win-states, fail closed)
userStories.description: Make canonical ELO purity a CI-enforced invariant, the way anonymity/segregation are. A ruleset may be CANONICAL only if its win-state space is exactly two mutually-exclusive, collectively-exhaustive TEAM outcomes (Town win XOR Mafia win, plus a defined draw handled per US-173) with no faction mutation, no solo/third faction, no alt-win condition, and no kingmaking objective.
acceptanceCriteria: A canonicality validator programmatically introspects every ruleset marked canonical and ASSERTS its terminal win-states reduce to exactly two MECE team outcomes (+ the defined draw); any solo faction, conversion/mutation, or alt-win condition fails the assertion.

CI gate test (extends the US-146 invariant suite) forbids ALL of: (a) any non-canonical-context game writing to canonical Rating/RatingEvent; (b) any rating event whose context is missing/None updating a canonical ladder; (c) any canonical ruleset that fails the two-MECE-outcomes introspection; (d) any canonical rating event missing ruleset_id/setup_id/rating_context_id/model+build ids/seed/team-outcome; (e) any canonical observation leaking model/provider/build identity; (f) any core role using wall-clock/unseeded-random/DB/network/chat-derived mechanics.
The gate runs in the default pytest -m 'not integration' suite and fails if any rule is violated.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Review (both Codex + Gemini): the canonical ladder must stay 'narrow, boring, heavily tested.' Unknown context => unrated, never canonical.

userStories.title: Deterministic endgame + MAX_DAYS tie-breaker + per-faction (Town/Scum) diagnostics
userStories.description: Both reviewers flagged the day-cap as a rating hazard (draw compresses ELO; survive-to-win breeds a stall meta; mafia-wins-on-clock breeds random lynching). Define one clean, deterministic terminal-resolution policy used identically across ALL canonical ladders, and track per-faction + per-role diagnostics so a blended ELO doesn't hide faction asymmetry.
acceptanceCriteria: A single documented deterministic terminal policy: normal parity/elimination win conditions, plus an explicit, mathematically clean MAX_DAYS outcome (town-win / mafia-win / draw) that is identical across every canonical ruleset and recorded in terminal_result.
If draws are possible, OpenSkill draw handling is applied consistently; a test asserts the day-cap outcome is deterministic and replay-stable.

Per-faction rating diagnostics: store Town-ELO and Scum-ELO (and per-role sample counts) separately even if a blended number is displayed; a model with N games shows its town/mafia/per-role sample breakdown.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Gemini: need a 'mathematically clean tie-breaker' to avoid degenerate metas. Codex: track town/mafia win rates + per-role sample counts as diagnostics, not just one blended ELO.

userStories.title: Night Action Resolution (NAR) tier system + resolution matrix
userStories.description: THE KEYSTONE. Replace ad-hoc night resolution with a formal, deterministic NAR tier system and an explicit resolution MATRIX (rows = action types; columns = blocked / protected / killed / redirected / watched-tracked / cleaned). Every current and future role's night interaction is defined by this matrix. Both reviewers: a flat prose order will rot; this must be a tested matrix with all edge cases pinned.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented tier order is implemented deterministically: (1) roleblocks, (2) redirects, (3) kills/protects/visits applied + visit-recording, (4) investigations/track/watch read resolved state, (5) death-reveal (with clean/forge handling). Pure-core, seed-driven, no wall-clock.

Explicit predeclared resolutions, each with a test: roleblock-vs-roleblock = BOTH blocks apply (no mutual-cancel special case); factional-kill ownership when the designated killer is roleblocked (define whether the kill fails or another scum carries it); whether a blocked action counts as a 'visit' for Tracker/Watcher; whether an investigation on a target that dies the same night still returns a result; structured deterministic FEEDBACK messages for blocked/protected/investigated outcomes (never inferred from chat).
A resolution-matrix data structure (or table) is the single source of truth; adding a role means adding matrix entries, not editing scattered conditionals.
Property test: replay-determinism holds across all role/action interaction combinations (Hypothesis stateful where practical).
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Codex: 'You need a resolution matrix, not prose.' Gemini: 'standard Natural Action Resolution (NAR) tier system' + redirect-vs-block + blocked-as-visit ambiguity. This story must merge BEFORE any new role.

userStories.title: Godfather (reads innocent to investigation) - first scum-skill role
userStories.description: The cleanest, lowest-variance scum deception role and the recommended FIRST addition. At investigation resolution, the Godfather returns Town/Innocent instead of true alignment. Deterministic single conditional; 2-faction-clean (Mafia-aligned). Tests information corruption / trust-calibration.
acceptanceCriteria: INVESTIGATE on the Godfather deterministically returns Innocent/Town; identity-blindness preserved (the result is falsified, not leaked). Resolved at the NAR investigation tier.
Variance guard: a test/diagnostic asserts the Detective still discriminates (the role corrupts a result, it must not make the Detective pure noise); document the measured effect on town inference.
Belongs only in 10p+ canonical setups (not mini7_v1).
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Codex: add Godfather FIRST; measure whether Detective still discriminates skill.

userStories.title: Mafia Roleblocker (action denial) on the NAR engine
userStories.description: Scum action-denial / tempo role. Blocks a target's active night action (passives immune). Uses the NAR tier-1 resolution. High skill (denying the town's power roles) but the biggest resolution-complexity contributor, so it lands only after the NAR matrix (US-174) and in a dedicated 10p+ setup.
acceptanceCriteria: ROLEBLOCK nullifies the target's active action per the NAR matrix; passives (e.g. Bulletproof/Mason) are immune; the roleblock-vs-roleblock and blocked-factional-killer cases follow US-174's predeclared rules with tests.
Deterministic structured feedback to the blocked actor; no chat-derived inference.
Introduced only in a dedicated 10p+ setup, not mini7_v1.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Codex: 'useful but dangerous... add Roleblocker in a dedicated setup.'

userStories.title: Janitor (suppress public role-reveal on death; leak-safe at game end)
userStories.description: Post-mortem information denial: on a successful kill the Janitor 'cleans' the death so the public death-reveal omits role/alignment. The true role is preserved internally for replay and the endgame reveal context. Must handle the win-condition-halt leak hazard the review raised.
acceptanceCriteria: On a cleaned death, the public death event omits role/alignment; the internal event state preserves the true role for deterministic replay and for the terminal endgame reveal.

LEAK GUARD: if a cleaned death triggers a terminal win condition (e.g. last mafia dies), the mid-game halt must NOT expose the cleaned role; the role is disclosed only via the terminal endgame-reveal projection (test this exact scenario).
X-shot capped (e.g. 1-shot) to bound variance; resolved at the NAR death-reveal tier.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Gemini: 'Janitor State Leaks' when a cleaned death ends the game. Codex: limit shots, be careful with observation redaction.

userStories.title: Framer (frame a town target as guilty) - variance-gated
userStories.description: The richest opponent-modeling scum role (predict the Detective's target) but the highest variance ('Framer = variance bomb' - N1 luck can lose town through no reasoning fault). Implement deterministically, but GATE its inclusion in any canonical ladder behind a measured variance budget.
acceptanceCriteria: FRAME marks a target; at the NAR investigation tier an investigation of a framed target deterministically returns Guilty/Mafia; framing resolves correctly when the Framer is itself roleblocked (per US-174).
X-shot/limited; identity-blind preserved.

VARIANCE GATE: the role is enabled in a canonical ruleset ONLY if a measured-variance check (e.g. games-to-converge / outcome-variance attributable to frame RNG vs skill) stays within an explicit budget documented in progress.txt; otherwise it ships disabled / experimental-only.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Both reviewers: Framer adds the most noise. Add Godfather + Roleblocker + Janitor first; admit Framer to canonical only if variance remains acceptable.

userStories.title: Canonical 'deception-hardened' 2-faction ruleset (10p+), vanilla-majority
userStories.description: Assemble the vetted scum-skill roles into a NEW canonical 2-faction ruleset at 11-13 players that benchmarks deception/info-denial/counter-deduction, while keeping power roles a minority so games stay skill-decided not power-decided.
acceptanceCriteria: A new ruleset (e.g. deception13_v1) registered via get_ruleset: ~25-30% scum, VANILLA-MAJORITY, one town info (Detective) + one town protection (Doctor) + Godfather + Mafia Roleblocker + Janitor; Framer included only if US-178's variance gate passed.
It declares a CANONICAL_TEAM rating context and PASSES the US-172 canonicality introspection (exactly two MECE team outcomes).
Power-role count stays a minority of seats; a balance note in progress.txt documents the town:scum ratio and power-role density vs the variance budget.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Both reviewers: do NOT put the quartet in mini7; role-heavy scored ladders want 10p+. Keep canonical ladders narrow and well-tested.

userStories.title: Paired/mirror games ('duplicate bridge') + balanced assignment + min-sample gates
userStories.description: Isolate logical skill from board RNG by exploiting Padrino's determinism: run the SAME seed twice with the model<->faction (and seat) assignment swapped, and aggregate the paired outcome into the canonical rating so 'got mafia more often' or N1-luck cancels out. Also enforce balanced role/seat assignment and minimum-sample gates before a model is ranked.
acceptanceCriteria: A paired-game scheduler produces mirror pairs from one seed (model A as Town / model B as Mafia, then swapped) deterministically; canonical rating updates consume the paired result so board-RNG/assignment is controlled for.

Role/seat assignment across a model's rated games is balanced (or paired) so per-(faction,role) sample counts are tracked; a model is shown as PROVISIONAL until per-context and per-faction minimum-sample gates are met.
Tests assert the mirror pairing is deterministic and that an unbalanced/under-sampled model is flagged provisional rather than ranked.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Gemini's 'Duplicate Bridge Solution' (CRITICAL) + Codex's seat/role-confounding warning. This is a high-leverage rating-quality win unique to a deterministic engine.

userStories.title: Deterministic, consistently-scored invalid-action handling
userStories.description: More roles => bigger structured-action space => more malformed/illegal action submissions. Define one deterministic fallback applied identically across all roles and ladders, counted in diagnostics so invalid-action rate is visible and never silently distorts ratings.
acceptanceCriteria: A malformed/illegal/empty structured action resolves to a single deterministic fallback (e.g. NOOP / forced-abstain) identical across roles; never randomized; never chat-derived.
Invalid-action rate per model is recorded as a diagnostic and surfaced; a test asserts the fallback is deterministic and replay-stable.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Codex: 'invalid action handling must be deterministic and scored consistently.'

userStories.title: PLACEMENT rating context (multi-team / solo-faction), firewalled from canonical
userStories.description: Scored context for modes with >2 outcome groups (e.g. Town vs Mafia vs Serial Killer). Use OpenSkill placement (winner ranked 1st, the rest tie) within that exact context only. Never writes to any canonical ladder.
acceptanceCriteria: A PLACEMENT rating context computes OpenSkill placement ratings from multi-team terminal results; it is its own context (per ruleset) and is CI-asserted non-canonical.
The US-172 gate confirms placement-context games write zero rows to canonical Rating/RatingEvent.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Both reviewers: OpenSkill natively handles multi-team placement; correct math for 3-faction/SK, but a SEPARATE context.

userStories.title: Serial Killer (solo faction) on a placement-scored ruleset
userStories.description: Implement the solo-killer faction (wins as last alive) ONLY inside a placement-scored, non-canonical ruleset. Tests solo survival + frame management + tempo. Never enters a canonical 2-faction ladder.
acceptanceCriteria: A 3-faction ruleset (Town vs Mafia vs SK) with deterministic SK night kill resolved via the NAR matrix; terminal results map to a placement outcome.
It declares a PLACEMENT context and FAILS the canonical introspection (proving the gate works); CI confirms zero canonical writes.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Gemini: native multi-team placement is the right model; keep larger setups to dampen its swing.

userStories.title: SOLO_RATE scoring context (Bayesian success rate + min-sample gates)
userStories.description: Scored context for alt-win roles whose objective is orthogonal to team play (Jester, Executioner, Survivor). Score them as per-role SUCCESS RATES with Bayesian/binomial credible intervals and minimum-sample gates - NOT ELO. Never writes to canonical or placement team ELO.
acceptanceCriteria: A SOLO_RATE context records per-role success/attempt counts and reports a success rate with a credible interval (e.g. Beta-binomial 95% CI); a role is shown only after a minimum-sample gate.
CI confirms SOLO_RATE games write zero rows to canonical Rating/RatingEvent and to placement ratings.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Both reviewers: 'Do not force ELO onto non-zero-sum dynamics'; report intervals + min-sample gates.

userStories.title: Jester (+ Executioner/Survivor) on SOLO_RATE-scored modes
userStories.description: Implement the alt-win roles whose entire value is a novel solo intelligence (Jester = anti-persuasion / reverse psychology), scored ONLY via the SOLO_RATE context (e.g. Jester lynch-bait success rate). Their alt-win conditions exist ONLY inside their own rulesets/contexts and never leak into team ELO.
acceptanceCriteria: Jester (win if day-voted out) implemented deterministically; its outcome records a SOLO_RATE success/attempt, never a team-ELO result; optionally Executioner (target lynched) and Survivor (survive to end) on the same context.
A test asserts a Jester/alt-win game produces zero canonical and zero placement-team rating writes (only a SOLO_RATE event).
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Directly answers the product question: Jester IS benchmark-worthy - as a standalone lynch-bait success rate, never inside Town-vs-Mafia ELO.

userStories.title: Multi-context presentation: separate cards, hard walls, no co-sorting
userStories.description: Surface every context's scores ('every mode scored') WITHOUT implying false comparability. Each model shows multiple context cards (canonical ELO per ruleset, placement ratings, solo success rates); they are never merged or sorted into one ranking; provisional/min-sample states are explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: The leaderboard/API exposes per-context, per-ruleset cards (e.g. 'bench10_v1 canonical: 31.2 +/-2.1', 'sk_10p placement: 24.8 +/-4.9', 'jester_8p lynch-bait: 42% CI 35-49'); canonical and non-canonical are never co-sorted in one list.
Hard separation in the surface (distinct sections/labels) marks canonical 'ranked' vs experimental contexts; provisional (under-sampled) models are labeled, not ranked.

If the spectator frontend is in scope, it renders the separated cards; otherwise the public API contract enforces the separation. All relevant gates (backend, and pnpm lint/check/test:e2e if frontend touched) pass.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Both reviewers: 'same model may have multiple cards, not one merged number'; 'never sort all of these together'; presentation must scream 'separate context.'

userStories.title: Tracker / Watcher (visit-graph inference) + Ninja (scum visit suppression)
userStories.description: Canonical-compatible town investigative roles that infer alignment from the night visit graph rather than handed-down labels - a distinct intelligence dimension. Tracker (whom target visited) and Watcher (who visited target), with Ninja as the scum counter (factional kill leaves no visit). All resolved via the NAR visit-recording tier.
acceptanceCriteria: Tracker and Watcher return deterministic visit lists per the NAR matrix; the blocked-action-as-visit rule (US-174) is honored; Ninja suppresses the killer's visit deterministically.
2-faction-clean; usable in 10p+ canonical setups; tests cover the block/ninja/visit interactions.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: DEFER until NAR is solid; Ninja only matters once Tracker/Watcher exist (bundle them).

userStories.title: Engine-stress utility roles: Mayor, Commuter, Mason
userStories.description: Lower-priority deterministic, 2-faction-clean roles that also exercise specific engine paths: Mayor (weighted vote -> tests tally/threshold logic), Commuter (untargetable at night -> tests invalid/no-op target bounce), Mason (confirmed-town pair -> coordination; channel firewalled, modeled as a shared fact not chat).
acceptanceCriteria: Mayor: deterministic public vote weight in the tally; a test covers threshold/hammer math with a weighted vote.
Commuter: untargetable resolution at the NAR tier; actions targeting a commuter resolve to the deterministic no-op/bounce (ties to US-181).
Mason: confirmed-town shared fact (no chat-driven mechanic); 2-faction-clean; identity-blind preserved.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Gemini: Mayor/Commuter are great engine-correctness tests. Mason is the cheapest coordination role; keep its channel firewalled.
